title: 10 best herbal tinctures for eye twitching
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for eye twitching are liquid extracts of herbs that are taken orally to alleviate eye twitching. These tinctures contain active compounds from herbs that help to reduce eye twitching by calming the nervous system and reducing stress, which are common triggers for eye twitching. Examples of herbs used in tinctures for eye twitching include passionflower, valerian root, and skullcap. By taking these tinctures, individuals can find relief from persistent eye twitching, improving their quality of life by reducing anxiety and discomfort associated with the condition. For instance, a person who frequently experiences eye twitching due to stress may find that taking a herbal tincture helps them to feel more relaxed and focused, allowing them to go about their daily activities without the distraction of eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Henbane tincture helps with eye twitching because it contains powerful alkaloids, such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine, that have antispasmodic and sedative properties. These alkaloids help to relax the muscles around the eye, reducing the frequency and intensity of eye twitches. Additionally, henbane tincture has been used traditionally to treat various nervous system disorders, including eye twitching, due to its ability to calm the nervous system and alleviate tension. However, it is important to note that henbane tincture should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner, as it can be toxic in large doses.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic activity: Henbane tincture contains alkaloids such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine, which block the action of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that can cause muscle spasms, including eye twitching.
Sedative effect: Henbane tincture has a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and twitching in the eyes, thanks to its alkaloid content.
Analgesic property: Henbane tincture has pain-relieving properties, which can help to alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with eye twitching, due to its anticholinergic and sedative effects.

Anti-inflammatory effect: Henbane tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce any swelling or inflammation that may be contributing to eye twitching, thanks to its alkaloid content.
Relaxant effect: Henbane tincture has a relaxant effect on smooth muscles, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and twitching in the eyes, due to its anticholinergic and sedative effects.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of fresh henbane leaves and stems, or 1 ounce of dried henbane, and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or grain alcohol over the henbane, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth, discarding the plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place. Take 1-2 drops up to three times daily, as needed for eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Belladonna tincture helps with eye twitching because it contains alkaloids that have antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce muscle spasms in the eyelid. The active compounds in belladonna, such as atropine and scopolamine, can help to relax the muscles around the eye and relieve tension. Additionally, belladonna has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce overall stress and anxiety that may be contributing to the eye twitching. However, it is important to note that belladonna should only be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can have serious side effects if used improperly.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic property: Belladonna tincture contains anticholinergic alkaloids such as atropine, scopolamine, and hyoscyamine, which help reduce muscle spasms and relax the eye muscles, thereby alleviating eye twitching.
Mydriatic property: Belladonna tincture can dilate the pupils by blocking the parasympathetic nervous system, which helps relieve tension and spasms in the eye muscles, reducing eye twitching.
Analgesic property: Belladonna tincture contains alkaloids with analgesic properties, such as hyoscyamine, which help reduce pain and discomfort associated with eye twitching.
Anti-inflammatory property: Belladonna tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eye muscles, thereby reducing eye twitching.

Sedative property: Belladonna tincture contains alkaloids with sedative properties, such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine, which help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, helping to alleviate eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 oz of dried belladonna herb into small pieces.
Combine herb and 1 pint of 100-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Seal jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks.
Shake the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid and store in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jimsonweed tincture helps with eye twitching because it contains scopolamine and hyoscyamine, alkaloids that have anticholinergic properties. These alkaloids can help reduce muscle spasms, including those that cause eye twitching. Jimsonweed tincture may also help alleviate eye twitching by relaxing the eye muscles and reducing inflammation in the eye area. Additionally, jimsonweed tincture may help improve overall eye health by promoting circulation and reducing eye strain. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using jimsonweed tincture or any other herbal remedy for eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Anti-spasmodic: Jimsonweed tincture helps alleviate eye twitching by relaxing the overactive muscles around the eye, thanks to its active constituents scopolamine and hyoscyamine.
Analgesic: The tincture reduces eye twitching discomfort by numbing the affected area, owing to its scopolamine and atropine components.
Sedative: Jimsonweed tincture helps relieve eye twitching by calming the nervous system, as it contains hyoscyamine and scopolamine that have sedative effects.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture helps reduce eye twitching by decreasing inflammation around the eye, containing active constituents like hyoscyamine and atropine.
Mydriatic: Jimsonweed tincture alleviates eye twitching by dilating the pupil, which can help relax the eye muscles, due to its scopolamine and atropine constituents.
remedy_recipe: Chop 20 gram of dried Jimsonweed herb, place it in a clean glass jar, then pour 200ml of 80-proof vodka or rum.
Tightly seal the jar, shake it well, and store in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks.
Shake the jar daily during the 2-week period.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid from the herb residue.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European death camas tincture helps with eye twitching because it contains the medicinal properties of the death camas plant. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for various ailments, including eye twitches. The tincture is made by soaking the plant's parts in alcohol, which extracts the active compounds. These compounds are believed to have a relaxing effect on the body, including the muscles around the eyes. By reducing the tension and spasms in the eye muscles, the tincture can help alleviate eye twitching. However, it is important to note that the effectiveness of this remedy may vary for different individuals, and it should not be used as a substitute for medical advice or treatment.
remedy_recipe: Chop 200 grams of European death camas roots into small pieces, ensuring they are similar in size.
Place the chopped roots in a clean glass jar, filling it up to two-thirds, then add 500ml of vodka or rum.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two weeks, shaking it gently every other day.
After two weeks, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or coffee filter, squeezing out the liquid into a clean glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the preparation date, dosage (5-10 drops), and store it in a cool, dark place for up to six months.
remedy_properties: Anti-spasmodic: European death camas tincture can help reduce eye twitching by relaxing the muscles around the eye, thanks to the alkaloids present in the plant.
Nervine: It can soothe the nervous system, which can help reduce eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety, due to the presence of nervine alkaloids.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture can reduce inflammation in the eye area, alleviating twitching caused by irritation or inflammation, due to the anti-inflammatory compounds like gallic acid.
Analgesic: It can relieve any pain or discomfort associated with eye twitching, thanks to the analgesic properties of the alkaloids present in the plant.
Sedative: European death camas tincture can help reduce eye twitching by promoting relaxation and sleep, due to the sedative alkaloids present in the plant.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Greater celandine tincture helps with eye twitching because it contains alkaloids such as chelidonine, which have antispasmodic properties. These properties can help to reduce muscle spasms in the eyelid, thereby alleviating eye twitching. Additionally, greater celandine has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help to soothe any irritation or discomfort that may be contributing to eye twitching. The herb's astringent properties may also help to tighten and tone the skin around the eye, reducing the likelihood of muscle spasms. Overall, greater celandine tincture can provide a natural and effective solution for treating eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Anti-spasmodic: Greater celandine tincture contains isoquinoline alkaloids, such as berberine and chelidonine, which help relax over-contracted eye muscles, reducing eye twitching.
Anti-inflammatory: Greater celandine has anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate eye inflammation and irritation, contributing to a reduction in eye twitching.
Analgesic: Containing alkaloids like sanguinarine, greater celandine tincture can help relieve minor eye pain, which can be associated with eye twitching.
Nervine: Greater celandine's calming effects on the nervous system can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to eye twitching.
Circulation-stimulating: Greater celandine can improve blood flow in the eye area, promoting proper oxygenation and nutrient supply, and supporting overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Chop fresh greater celandine herb (consisting of leaves, stems, and roots) into small pieces, filling a clean, dry glass jar up to half.
Pour 80-proof vodka into the jar, ensuring the herb is completely submerged and leaving a 1-inch space at the top.
Seal the jar tightly and place it in a cool, dark area for two to four weeks, shaking the mixture gently every few days.
After four weeks, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out the liquid from the herb residue.
Label the bottle with the preparation date and store it in a cool, dark place; take 20-30 drops of the tincture, three times a day, for eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tincture helps with eye twitching because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the eye area. The herb's active compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, may help to strengthen the eye muscles and improve circulation to the eye, reducing the frequency and intensity of eye twitching. Additionally, eyebright has been traditionally used to treat various eye conditions, including conjunctivitis and styes, due to its natural antibacterial and antiviral properties. By promoting overall eye health and reducing inflammation, eyebright tincture can help alleviate eye twitching and improve eye comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its tannins and flavonoids, helps reduce eye twitching caused by inflammation or irritation.
Astringent: The astringent property of eyebright, attributed to its tannins, helps tighten and soothe the skin around the eye, potentially reducing muscle spasms that cause twitching.
Antioxidant: Eyebright's antioxidant constituents, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the eye area from oxidative stress and may help alleviate eye twitching.
Analgesic: Eyebright tincture's analgesic property, owing to its alkaloids and phenolic acids, can help relieve any discomfort or pain associated with eye twitching.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic effect of eyebright, attributed to its alkaloids and volatile oils, helps relax the eye muscles, potentially reducing eye twitching frequency and duration.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried eyebright herb, place it in a clean glass jar, then pour 16 ounces of vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it's fully submerged.
Securely fasten a lid on the jar, then store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
After 2-4 weeks, strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out the liquid from the herb residue.
Discard the used herb and store the tincture in a cool, dark place; it will last for several years.
To use the tincture for eye twitching, dilute 20-30 drops in a quarter cup of water and use an eyedropper to apply 1-2 drops of the solution to the affected eye, 2-3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with eye twitching because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eye area. Plantain is rich in nutrients such as vitamin A, vitamin C, and potassium, which are essential for maintaining healthy eye function. The tincture can help soothe and relax the eye muscles, reducing the frequency and intensity of eye twitching. Additionally, plantain has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. By promoting overall eye health and reducing inflammation, plantain tincture can help alleviate eye twitching and improve eye comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce eye twitching by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids, tannins, and saponins.
Astringent: Its astringent properties, due to tannins, help tighten and tone the skin and muscles around the eye, reducing twitching.
Analgesic: Plantain tincture alleviates eye twitching by providing pain relief, owing to its active constituents such as flavonoids, volatile oils, and phenolic acids.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of plantain tincture, derived from its active constituents like flavonoids and volatile oils, help relax the involuntary muscle spasms causing eye twitching.

Nervine: Plantain tincture's nervine properties, due to its active constituents like flavonoids, tannins, and volatile oils, help soothe the nervous system, reducing eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Chop fresh plantain leaves, filling a clean, dry mason jar up to 3/4 full.
Pour 100-proof vodka over the leaves, ensuring they are completely submerged, filling the jar to the top.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
After 4-6 weeks, strain the mixture through cheesecloth, discarding the spent leaves.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle, using it as needed (a few drops in the eye) for eye twitching relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rue tincture helps with eye twitching because it contains alkaloids and essential oils that have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. These components can help reduce muscle spasms and inflammation in the eye area, providing relief from eye twitching. Rue tincture may also improve blood circulation and nerve function, which can further alleviate eye twitching. Additionally, rue tincture has been traditionally used as a natural remedy for various eye conditions, including eye strain and fatigue, which can also contribute to eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Rue tincture can help reduce eye twitching by relaxing the muscles around the eye, thanks to the presence of active constituents like alkaloids and essential oils.
Nervine: Rue tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety, due to the presence of volatile oils and alkaloids.
Anti-inflammatory: Rue tincture can help reduce inflammation in the eye area, which may help alleviate eye twitching, thanks to the presence of active compounds like flavonoids and essential oils.
Analgesic: Rue tincture can help alleviate pain associated with eye twitching, thanks to the presence of active constituents like alkaloids, flavonoids, and essential oils.
Circulatory stimulant: Rue tincture can help improve blood flow to the eye area, which may help reduce eye twitching, thanks to the presence of active constituents like alkaloids and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Chop one ounce of dried rue herb into small pieces.
Place herb in a clean glass jar, then pour 2 cups of vodka or rum over it.
Securely fasten a lid on the jar and let it steep for two to four weeks in a cool, dark place.
Shake the jar daily to help release the herb's properties.
Strain the mixture and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle. Take 5-10 drops of the tincture up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with eye twitching because it contains antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate muscle spasms and inflammation in the eye area. Chamomile's antispasmodic properties can help reduce the frequency and intensity of eye twitching, while its anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe any irritation or inflammation that may be contributing to the twitching. Additionally, chamomile contains flavonoids that have a relaxing effect on the body, which can help reduce overall tension and stress that may be exacerbating the eye twitching. By addressing both the physical and mental factors that can contribute to eye twitching, chamomile tincture can provide a holistic and natural approach to managing this common and often frustrating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its chamazulene and bisabolol content, helps reduce eye twitching caused by inflammation.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic property, attributed to the presence of bisabolol and matricin, aids in alleviating eye twitching by relaxing the involuntary muscle spasms.
Analgesic: German chamomile tincture's analgesic effect, owing to its chamazulene and flavonoids, assists in minimizing eye twitching discomfort and pain.
Sedative: The sedative property, resulting from the presence of apigenin and alpha-bisabolol, helps alleviate eye twitching by promoting relaxation and reducing stress-induced muscle spasms.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tincture's antioxidant property, attributed to its flavonoids and essential oils, aids in protecting the eyes from oxidative stress and damage that can contribute to eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried German chamomile flowers, placing them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of 100-proof vodka over the chopped flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth, discarding the spent flowers and storing the liquid in a dark glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the preparation date and dosage instructions (e.g., 20-30 drops in water, up to three times a day).

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tincture helps with eye twitching because it contains passiflora incarnata, a plant that has antispasmodic properties. These properties can help relax the muscles around the eye, reducing the frequency and intensity of eye twitching. Additionally, maypop tincture may also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can be triggers for eye twitching. The herb's sedative effects can promote calmness and relaxation, further alleviating eye twitching. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before taking maypop tincture or any other herbal remedy.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Maypop tincture can help reduce eye twitching by relaxing the muscles around the eye, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and alkaloids.
Sedative property: This tincture can help alleviate eye twitching by reducing nervousness and promoting relaxation, due to its active compounds such as chrysin and isorhamnetin.
Analgesic property: Maypop tincture can help manage eye twitching by reducing any potential pain or discomfort, as it contains active alkaloids like passionflorine and harmine.
Antioxidant property: This tincture can help protect the eye area from damage and irritation, thus reducing eye twitching, due to its rich antioxidant content like flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Anti-inflammatory property: Maypop tincture can help alleviate eye twitching by reducing inflammation and swelling in the eye area, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 oz of dried maypop herb, place it in a clean jar, then fill the jar with 80-proof vodka until the herb is fully submerged.
Tightly seal the jar, shake it well, then store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it daily.
After 2-4 weeks, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid from the herb residue.
Discard the herb residue and transfer the resulting liquid to a clean, amber-colored glass bottle.
Take 30-60 drops of the maypop tincture, 3 times a day, for eye twitching, storing the bottle in a cool, dark place when not in use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye twitching with herbal tinctures is by using a tincture of passionflower and/or valerian root. These herbs have calming properties that can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for eye twitching. Take 30 drops of the tincture, mixed in a small amount of water, three times a day. Additionally, consider taking milk thistle tincture to support liver function, as liver imbalances can also cause eye twitching. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye twitching that are treated with herbal tinctures are blepharitis and conjunctivitis. Blepharitis is an inflammation of the eyelids, characterized by soreness, itching, and crusty eyelashes. Conjunctivitis, also known as pink eye, is an inflammation or infection of the conjunctiva, the clear membrane that lines the eyelid and eye surface. Herbal tinctures like eyebright, goldenseal, and chamomile are used to alleviate symptoms of these conditions due to their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and soothing properties. However, it is crucial to consult a healthcare professional before starting any herbal treatment.
